lockStatus.notes: Civilian names and final casting details can lock at script without breaking structure.

organization.name: Lattice
organization.roleInSeason: Primary antagonist force — surveillance, recruitment pressure, direct action, and continuity research tied to Wraith's origin.

henchmenAndSquadTemplates.purpose: Episodes 6+ need repeatable mooks with rules so fights stay authored — not random armor guys.

latticeGrunt.label: Lattice Grunt (Uniformed operative)
latticeGrunt.visualHook: Matte armor, soft joints — built for crowd control, not duels. Face obscured; numbers on shoulder for storytelling clarity (who falls, who talks).
latticeGrunt.behavior: Follows radio doctrine; breaks when leadership breaks. Not brave — trained.

latticeElite.label: Lattice Elite (Breacher)
latticeElite.visualHook: Heavier plates, distinct silhouette — reads as 'mini-boss' in groups of 2–3 max.
latticeElite.behavior: Authorized for capture missions; tries to split the team spatially.

rulesForMooks: Mooks talk in procedure — callouts, clearance codes — never monologue.
If a mook removes a helmet, give them one human line max — then consequence — so violence has weight.
Lattice does not infinite-spawn; show logistics (vans, timing) so Nova's wins feel earned.

continuityChecks.episode5: Echo must read as controlled — Solace's observation must be fair on rewatch. See characters/antagonists/echo.json → continuityNote
continuityChecks.episode9: Architect motivation must match Season 1/season.json villain block verbatim in spirit. See characters/antagonists/the-architect.json → motivation
continuityChecks.episode12: Architect defeated but not destroyed — no accidental death fakeout unless scripted with purpose. See characters/antagonists/the-architect.json → outcome
